absolute_scale: requires competing weights and dimensionful kappa ratios such as kappa_1/kappa_0; those are unsourced primitives
claim_boundary: v6.0.2 identifies the smallest finite parent-action families permitted by explicit covariance and differential-order assumptions. BHSM does not select their coefficients, signature, confinement invariant, or a stable physical phase.
flat_direction: if only one homogeneous weight is nonzero, the overall scaling equation cannot select an isolated finite L without additional zero/constraint structure
hessian: second derivatives of the displayed S_red; entries not numerical
ratio_fixing: possible from dimensionless F_k competition even at one scale weight

stationarity.collar: no independent equation without interface action
